body.title: [sprint] Per-ticket token/usage attribution + store — metrics substrate for cost-aware builders
body.description:
**Problem:** Cost-aware builders need to know what a ticket will cost before starting (the 'don't start what you can't finish' intention) and the system needs a shared burn view ('inference is a shared resource'). Both bottom out in ONE missing primitive: per-ticket token/usage ACTUALS, attributed to a ticket id and stored. Today budget/burn tools exist (budget_remaining, openrouter_burn_rate, budget_summary) but usage is NOT attributed per ticket — so we can neither build an estimator (no history of actuals) nor a per-ticket circuit breaker. This is the critical-path unblocker for the whole builder-orchestration line (DS-as-primary, CC.1-as-overflow, cost-aware claim gates).

**Proposed shape:** Capture per-ticket usage actuals (tokens in/out, provider, worker, wall-clock, $ where derivable) attributed to the ticket id, and store them durably in Postgres keyed by ticket id. EXTENDS the existing budget/burn infra; does NOT rebuild it. Measurement substrate ONLY — the estimator and the gates are downstream tickets.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint. Likely: the sprint-ticket lifecycle (start/close) to bracket a ticket's usage window; the budget/burn tooling (openrouter_burn_rate / budget_summary) as the usage source; a new devlab table (e.g. devlab.ticket_usage) via the migration path.
**Design rules:** NO SQLITE (Postgres or flat-file only); External State Principle (usage stored durably, queryable across sessions/builders); log the interface crossing when usage is attributed (AR-009).
**Scope boundary:** IN — attribute + store per-ticket usage actuals keyed by ticket id; make them queryable by id and in aggregate. OUT — the cost ESTIMATOR (downstream); the pre-claim GATE + burn circuit breaker (downstream); DS promotion/trust-tracking (separate); cross-provider $ normalization beyond what budget tools already give.
**Test plan:** Run a ticket start->close; assert a usage row is written keyed to its ticket id with non-zero tokens + provider + worker; re-query returns it by id and in aggregate; works for a CC ticket (and a DS ticket when DS is available).

**Completion criteria:**
- Per-ticket usage actuals (tokens, provider, worker, wall-clock) are captured and stored in Postgres keyed by ticket id at ticket close.
- The stored actuals are queryable by ticket id AND in aggregate (so a future estimator has a history of actuals).
- No SQLite anywhere; the usage store survives a session restart.
- Estimator + claim-gate + burn-breaker explicitly NOT implemented (downstream tickets).
body.intention: I intend that every ticket's actual token/usage cost is measured and stored attributed to its ticket id, so the system can later estimate ticket cost up front and treat inference as a shared, monitored resource.
